\documentclass[11pt]{article}
\usepackage{amsmath}
\usepackage{booktabs}
\pagestyle{empty}
\begin{document}

\section*{Synthetic Fixture}

This tiny document exists only to exercise the conversion pipeline. It contains
one display equation and one table so the integration test can assert that the
converter produces at least one \texttt{FORMULA} node and at least one table node.

The Gaussian integral has the well-known closed form shown below:

\begin{equation}
\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} e^{-\alpha x^{2}} \, dx
  = \sqrt{\frac{\pi}{\alpha}}, \qquad \alpha > 0 .
\end{equation}

Table~\ref{tab:results} reports a few synthetic measurements.

\begin{table}[h]
\centering
\begin{tabular}{lrr}
\toprule
Method & Accuracy & Time (s) \\
\midrule
Baseline & 0.71 & 12.4 \\
Improved & 0.84 & 15.9 \\
Proposed & 0.92 & 11.2 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\caption{Synthetic results for the test fixture.}
\label{tab:results}
\end{table}

\end{document}
